score_notes.Baseline Coverage: Draft covers baseline topics present in SERP corpus.
score_notes.SERP Novelty: 13 delta win(s) vs corpus; 10 high-novelty sentences.

ig_why_summary.one_liner: This draft wins on information gain because it is not a connect-in-five-minutes tutorial — it documents a least-privilege, block-aware, preview-gated publish pipeline on a real self-hosted site, with named abilities, explicit omissions, and verification steps page-one does not assemble.
ig_why_summary.headline: 12/12 — publish ready
score_rationale: Delta Fidelity: Draft reflects 3/3 delta titles.
Baseline Coverage: Draft covers baseline topics present in SERP corpus.
SERP Novelty: 13 delta win(s) vs corpus; 10 high-novelty sentences.
Intent Fit: Navigational intent served (6 wiring/install signals).
Risk Honesty: Limits and caveats appear stated.

wins_vs_serp.title: Curated least-privilege abilities
wins_vs_serp.body: Page-one promotes broad MCP/REST exposure and plugin roundups; the draft documents a bounded rootsandfruit/* surface, permission basis, and explicit omissions (no delete, no cache purge).

wins_vs_serp.title: Guarded publish path
wins_vs_serp.body: Competitors stop at connect-and-edit; the draft adds a preview-gated checklist with set-post-author, Breeze purge, and enable-public-preview before publish-post.

wins_vs_serp.title: Block-first body rules
wins_vs_serp.body: Generic results say create/update posts; the draft separates blocks-* for body vs update-post for title/excerpt and documents the innerHTML failure mode.

wins_vs_serp.title: Single MCP architecture
wins_vs_serp.body: Listicles offer many servers; the draft commits to one Cursor MCP, in-process blocks, and explicit routing rules.

wins_vs_serp.title: Live operator evidence
wins_vs_serp.body: Vendor docs and AI summaries lack verifiable solo-operator detail; the draft names smoke scripts, plugin versions, and a real self-hosted stack.

ig_why_summary.page_one_contrast: A **WordPress MCP (Model Context Protocol) Server** `bridges AI assistants (like Claude, Cursor, and VS Code) with your WordPress website so you can manage content, posts, pages, and themes directly via natural language`.[](https://developer.wordpress.org/news/2026/02/from-abilities-to-ai-agents-introducing-the-wordpress-mcp-adapter/) [[1]](https://developer.wordpress.org/news/2026/02/from-abiliti
notable_novel_claims: I run [rootsandfruit.com](https://rootsandfruit.com/) as a one-person shop: self-hosted WordPress on Cloudways, Kadence, Breeze cache.
From there I split responsibilities on purpose: block work goes through `rootsandfruit/blocks-*`; title and excerpt go through `rootsandfruit/update-post`.
Generic MCP setups often mirror large parts of the REST API.
**What I deliberately left out:** post delete, trash, cache purge.
Breeze cache clears stay manual—I purge after byline changes so logged-out preview matches what I expect.
caveats: Score is corpus-backed and deterministic — not LLM/embeddings. Optional: mcp.json example for copy-paste navigational intent.
